mechanism_narrative: A low and stable FEDFUNDS rate anchors short rates and supports equity duration by keeping discount rates compressed, so the S&P 500 tends to rise over the next month when policy stays unchanged and inflation is not accelerating. This effect fails if CPIAUCSL re-accelerates enough to force a repricing of higher future rates.
failure_condition: Reject if, during periods with flat FEDFUNDS and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL, 20-day SPX returns are negative in a majority of cases or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent-month threshold signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which reduces expected earnings and pushes the S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal should strengthen when PAYEMS is also falling, confirming broad labor weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a 0.3pp UNRATE rise do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if PAYEMS declines fail to add explanatory power relative to UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumption demand and revenue growth, which supports earnings expectations and lifts the S&P 500 over the next month. The effect should weaken if retail sales rise while unemployment is simultaneously increasing, since that would imply inflationary stress rather than healthy demand.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint RSAFS-UNRATE interaction does not outperform RSAFS alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising from a soft base, it signals improving industrial output and easing supply constraints, which should support cyclical equities and lift the S&P 500 over the next month. The mechanism fails if output gains are accompanied by a sharp rise in CPIAUCSL, because higher inflation would dominate the favorable supply signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month over month while CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if months with rising INDPRO and stable CPIAUCSL do not show positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not statistically better than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST reflects weakening housing demand and tighter rate sensitivity, which propagates into consumer spending and lowers the S&P 500 over the next week. This signal is strongest when GDPC1 is also soft, indicating broad demand deceleration rather than a temporary housing-specific lull.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if GDPC1 does not improve classification versus HOUST alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate in a tightening cycle increases discount rates and pressure on equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is expected to be stronger when CPIAUCSL is also rising, because inflation persistence makes future hikes more likely.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 25 bps within 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if CPIAUCSL does not materially strengthen the signal relative to FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS reading signals payroll contraction and weaker aggregate income, which reduces risk appetite and pushes the S&P 500 lower over the next quarter. The relationship should be most reliable when UNRATE is also rising, confirming that the labor deterioration is broad-based rather than a noisy payroll print.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction fails to improve out-of-sample hit rate versus PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in GDPC1 after a weak prior quarter indicates improving final demand and supports earnings expectations, lifting the S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days. This trend signal fails when GDP growth is accompanied by rising unemployment, because the growth impulse then reflects unstable or backward-looking revisions rather than genuine demand strength.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 grows faster than its prior quarter and UNRATE is not rising
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 improvement does not translate into positive 60-day SPX returns in most cases, or if the interaction with UNRATE provides no incremental predictive value.
